subject: [PATCH v2 0/3] mm: improve write performance with RWF_DONTCACHE

reviews.author: Ritesh (IBM)
reviews.summary: Ritesh suggested an alternative approach to Christoph's suggestion, proposing to track the number of dontcache pages and kick the flusher for those many target pages, which would still reduce dirty page cache pressure without necessarily writing only dontcache marked folios.
reviews.raw_body:
So, if I understood it correctly, Christoph might be talking about a
different approach here.
Instead of kicking flusher thread to writeback pages for a single inode,
if we can track the number of dontcache pages
(get_nr_dontcache_pages()), then we can kick the flusher for those many
target pages. I think this way we are still reducing the dirty page
cache pressure - the problem which RWF_DONTCACHE is supposed to solve.
But I guess, that doesn't necessarily always mean that only dontcache
marked folios will get written.

If we implement that then, this should still help with the batching
problem you mentioned and hopefully should not cause a major regression
for the workload which Jan mentioned.

Feel free to correct my understanding here please.

-ritesh

2026-04-17.patch_summary: This patch set improves write performance when using the RWF_DONTCACHE flag by kicking the flusher thread for the superblock instead of initiating writeback directly. This change is based on benchmarking results that show a clear improvement over the status quo, particularly in cases with heavy write activity. The patch also includes a comparison of different IO modes for nfsd and highlights the poor performance of RWF_DONTCACHE with write-heavy workloads. The approach taken involves modifying the generic_write_sync function to kick the flusher thread, which is expected to better isolate the DONTCACHE writeback behavior and improve overall performance.
